_note: Refreshed by the refresh-sports-feed scheduled task. Each item is {headline, url, source, published (ISO8601), snark}. Snark voice: Barstool-adjacent guy-at-the-bar, crass, horny, cocky, zero filler. One sentence. No slurs, no punching down at specific real people in a harmful way. Punch sideways (at media, front offices, rival fanbases) and up (at ownership). Keep it Boston.

items.headline: Roman Anthony out with sore back, day-to-day for series finale vs Yankees
items.snark: Top prospect's back seized up Wednesday so now we're trotting out Andruw Monasterio like it's 2019 again, cool cool cool.

items.headline: Sonny Gray hamstring strain less serious than feared, eligible May 6 return
items.snark: MRI came back Grade 1, so Gray dodged a bullet but this rotation is still held together with duct tape and prayers.

items.headline: Eduardo Rivera optioned to Worcester after impressive 3.1 scoreless inning debut
items.snark: Rivera struck out three Yankees then got sent down faster than a bad Tinder date, because apparently we can't have nice things.

items.headline: Chad Tracy drawing endorsements for permanent manager job from Athletic columnist
items.snark: Tracy's getting the interim manager kiss of death treatment where everyone says nice things before ownership hires some retread.

items.headline: Red Sox pitching good enough to win but offense isn't
items.snark: Staff ERA is elite but the bats couldn't score in a strip club with Henry's credit card, which is the 2026 Sox in a nutshell.

items.headline: Red Sox 4, Royals 3: Duran's two-run homer rallies Boston past KC
items.snark: Duran finally remembered he plays baseball and hit a timely bomb, which is more than the other seven guys in the lineup can say.

items.headline: Red Sox prospect Bryce Bennett strikes out 11 in Triple-A Worcester gem
items.snark: Number six prospect is carving up Triple-A hitters while the big club can't hit water falling out of a boat, call him up you cowards.

items.headline: Red Sox late-inning bullpen owns MLB's best ERA, batting average against since Opening Day
items.snark: Slaten, Whitlock, Chapman are 19-for-19 holding leads after seven, shame the offense can't get them one.

items.headline: Patriots scouting report: Drake Maye undisputed face of franchise entering Year 2
items.snark: Maye's the franchise now which is great except he's got no receivers and the OL gave up eight pressures in the Super Bowl.

items.headline: Patriots add DT Travis Shaw as UDFA, release long snapper Niko Lalos
items.snark: Signed a Texas defensive tackle nobody's heard of and cut the long snapper, which means absolutely nothing until September.

items.headline: Insider: NFC contender could emerge as competition for Patriots in AJ Brown pursuit
items.snark: AJ Brown trade rumors hit Week 47 and now some mystery NFC team wants in, which means Philly's asking price just went up again.
